raw_output:
PR #5 security review (Claude, Fable 5, independent fresh-context pass, lane-B range at dd565f3).

Findings: S1 (low) Phase 5 report convention widens the sanctioned unreviewed-content channel beyond its own "same as any delta note" equivalence claim — report is unbounded planned prose no leg sees; delta-note mechanism undefined; nothing requires the committed copy to match the delivered message. Kept low: fully-malicious-orchestrator exploitation adds nothing over the artifact-genuineness residual; the report is by design human-read; the never-in-hash-exempt-path hardening is correct. S2 (low) THREAT_MODEL claims dev/CI jobs hold "no secrets"; gitleaks step has GITHUB_TOKEN in env, lychee receives github.token implicitly — internally inconsistent (the next sentence admits token exfiltration). S3 (low) tag-pinning claimed as a Threat-1 control; no tags exist (verified empty local and remote). S4 (low) ADR 0004 claims Socket heuristics; no in-repo evidence (grep: this ADR only). S5 (info) "none of which execute downloaded code" checkably false as worded — the gate job runs SHA-pinned actions/checkout and actions/setup-python; only the script is hermetic. S6 (info) the workflow/gate-script always-escalates rule is promoted to an unqualified "control" while enforcement is procedural (orchestrator adherence + review), with no CODEOWNERS or path-scoped mechanism.

Verified sound: ALLOWED_MODELS matches ADR 0002; fail_under/branch matches ADR 0005; pins match ADR 0004 narrative; base-branch-copy claim matches ci.yml; escalation list unchanged; never-in-hash-exempt-path rule is genuine hardening. Overall: no critical/high; nothing weakens an enforced control or gives consumer agents dangerous instructions; the S2-S5 pattern is describing intended rather than shipped posture.

--- Post-review delta note (orchestrator, attested at final head 83d6f55) ---
The four legs reviewed the lane-B range at dd565f3 (stacked on unmerged lane
A). Single round, zero blocking findings. What changed after the legs ran:
(1) fix batch 9cc8a41/1a1e99a closing every validated finding — docs
rewritten from intended posture to shipped state (tags marked nonexistent
pending a release decision; advisory jobs labeled advisory; "no secrets"
corrected to the ephemeral read-only token; gate-job hermeticity narrowed to
the stdlib-only script + SHA-pinned first-party actions; Socket attributed to
its GitHub App install; escalation rule labeled procedural), Phase 5
equivalence claim replaced with an explicit wider-window concession + a
committed-report-matches-delivered-message requirement, delta notes given a
definition in the process doc, mutation-check precedent re-attributed to
PR #1, Considered Options added to ADRs 0004/0005, wave-1 report blocking
count clarified; (2) rebase onto main after PR #4 merged (lane-B content
unchanged; range diff now equals the main...HEAD diff this hash covers);
(3) the wave-2 report committed per the Phase 5 option-C convention this PR
itself introduces — the report matches the message delivered to Kristov.
Verification on the final head: markdown-only diff (no code paths), all
relative links resolve, git diff --check clean, full test suite still 58
passed with the 99 floor. Why no re-run: every change is finding-responsive
doc correction or the process records the convention prescribes; no code,
gate, CI, or instruction-semantics change beyond the reviewed Phase 5 text
whose final wording implements the Claude security leg's own S1 remedy.

findings.summary: Phase 5 report convention wider than its delta-note equivalence claim; delta note undefined; no message-faithfulness requirement
findings.reason: Equivalence claim removed — Phase 5 now concedes the wider window explicitly; committed report must match the delivered message (mismatch = artifact fabrication); delta notes defined in docs/process/review-gate.md (required content, bounds, when re-run is mandatory instead). Codex security judged the channel within the existing residual; the wording fixes stand on their own.

findings.summary: 'No secrets' claim false: scanner steps hold the ephemeral read-only token
findings.reason: THREAT_MODEL reworded to the actual token posture; conclusion (cannot push/approve/merge) unchanged and correct.

findings.summary: Tag-pinning claimed as a control; no tags exist
findings.reason: Listed as planned/not-yet-operative in THREAT_MODEL; first cut escalated in the wave-2 report.

findings.summary: Socket heuristics claimed without in-repo evidence
findings.reason: Severity adjusted: the control exists (account-level GitHub App; check runs on every PR) — the defect was provenance documentation, now fixed in ADR 0004 and the standards table.

findings.summary: 'None of which execute downloaded code' overstates gate-job hermeticity
findings.reason: Narrowed: the enforcement script is hermetic; the job runs SHA-pinned first-party actions (checkout, setup-python). Same fix applied to ADR 0004.

findings.summary: Trust-boundary escalation presented as unqualified control; enforcement is procedural
findings.reason: THREAT_MODEL now labels it a procedural control (orchestrator adherence + review, no mechanical implementation), pointing at threat 2's residuals.
